rag_rules: Use validator locations to make the smallest local edit first; a parse error at a token often means the previous AGREE statement is unterminated or incomplete.
When a diagnostic indicates an unresolved name or invalid helper, fix the root reference if it is already visible; otherwise remove only the offending clause instead of adding architecture.
If a repair touches temporal logic with pre(), keep or add proper initialization so previous-state references are well formed.
generic_rules: Resolve the first reported validator error before changing later lines, since downstream diagnostics may be cascading.
Preserve unrelated declarations, connections, and component structure; repair only the text directly implicated by the diagnostic.
Keep repairs within existing visible symbols and local helper declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: End each AGREE clause or declaration with a semicolon.
Ensure helper declarations are type-consistent with their expressions, and use initialized recurrence for stateful equations.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or widen scope just to silence an AGREE error.
Do not leave an invalid clause in place if its identifiers or property names are not actually available in AGREE expression scope.
